'''Ben Currie''' (born 15 July 1994) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]]. He has played for the [[England Knights]], [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

Currie has spent time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Currie was born in [[Lowton]], [[Greater Manchester, England]].
He attended Golborne High School for his secondary education

==Club career==
Currie played his junior rugby at Golborne Parkside before signing with the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Warrington Wolves' Ben Currie set to add more spice to Super League|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/10199241.Currie_set_to_add_more_spice_to_Super_League|website=Warrington Guardian|access-date=11 December 2017|date=1 February 2013}}</ref> He made his début for Warrington in April 2012, aged 17, in a 46–12 victory over the [[Widnes Vikings]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Warrington roar back to the top after early scare against Widnes|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/apr/05/warrington-widnes-super-league|website=The Guardian|access-date=11 December 2017|date=5 April 2012}}</ref> He scored his first senior try a month later against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]].<ref>{{cite web|last1=Hadfield|first1=Dave|title=Brave Salford find Warrington too hot with Currie|website=[[Independent.co.uk]] |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/brave-salford-find-warrington-too-hot-with-currie-7718626.html|access-date=11 December 2017|date=6 May 2012}}</ref>

In 2013, he played one game for [[Swinton Lions]] on a dual-registration deal, scoring two tries against the [[Sheffield Eagles]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Warrington Wolves' Ben Currie strikes twice on Swinton Lions début|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/10233898.Currie_strikes_twice_on_Swinton_Lions_debut/?ref=arc|website=Warrington Guardian|access-date=11 December 2017|date=17 February 2013}}</ref> He made two further appearances for Swinton in 2014, scoring a hat trick against [[Barrow Raiders]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Swinton Lions 44-26 Barrow Raiders|url=http://www.rugby-league.com/championship__league1/match_report/10131|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171211161134/http://www.rugby-league.com/championship__league1/match_report/10131|url-status=dead|archive-date=11 December 2017|publisher=The Rugby Football League|access-date=11 December 2017|date=23 March 2014}}</ref>

In June 2015, Currie signed a new four-year contract with Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|title=Ben Currie: Warrington Wolves forward signs new contract|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/33167372|website=BBC Sport|access-date=11 December 2017|date=17 June 2015}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

In September 2016, Currie suffered a torn [[anterior cruciate ligament]], ruling him out for most of the 2017 season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Ben Currie: Warrington Wolves forward suffers serious knee injury|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37429806|website=BBC Sport|date=21 September 2016|access-date=11 December 2017}}</ref> Despite the injury, he was named in the 2016 [[Super League Dream Team]] for his performances during the season, and was rewarded with a new contract at Warrington, keeping him at the club until 2020.<ref>{{cite web|title=Warrington Wolves: Chris Hill and Ben Currie agree new contracts|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37922493|website=BBC Sport|access-date=11 December 2017|date=9 November 2016}}</ref>

He returned to action in July 2017, but suffered another injury to the same knee in his second game after returning to the side. He was able to recover before the end of the season, playing two games for Warrington in the [[2017 Super 8s|2017 Qualifiers]].{{cn|date=October 2022}}

He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>

Currie was granted a testimonial by Warrington, which took place prior to the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Warrington Wolves 22 Leigh Leopards 10, Ben Currie Testimonial |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/reports/23299905.warrington-wolves-22-leigh-leopards-10-ben-currie-testimonial/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=17 September 2023 |date=4 February 2023}}</ref>
Currie played 28 games for Warrington in the 2023 Super League season as Warrington finished sixth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. Currie played in the clubs elimination playoff loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23827325.highs-lows-warrington-wolves-2023-season/|title=Highs and lows from Warrington Wolves' 2023 season|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk|date=3 October 2023 }}</ref>
On 8 June 2024, Currie played in Warrington's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] defeat against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 7 June 2025, Currie played in Warrington's 8-6 Challenge Cup final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==International career==
Currie made his international début for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]], and played in all three of their group games.

In 2015, Currie was selected by Steve McNamara to represent [[England national rugby league team|England]] for their [[2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|test series against New Zealand]] but did not make an appearance.<ref>{{cite news | title = MCNAMARA NAMES 24-MAN SQUAD FOR 2015 INTERNATIONAL SERIES | publisher = englandrl.co.uk | date = 2015-10-11 | url = http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article/47305/mcnamara-names-24-man-squad-for | access-date = 2015-10-24}}</ref>

In 2017, he was one of two uncapped players to be named in England's [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]] squad.<ref name="England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-roby-back-fold-wayne-11312505|title=England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold|publisher=Mirror|date=9 October 2017|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Ben Currie and Alex Walmsley named in England's Rugby League World Cup squad|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/15314/11073684/ben-currie-and-alex-walmsley-named-in-englands-rugby-league-world-cup-squad|website=Sky Sports|access-date=10 January 2018|date=9 October 2017}}</ref> He made his début for England in the opening game against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] as a substitute after being named in the team as a late replacement for [[Alex Walmsley]], who was ruled out due to illness.<ref>{{cite web|title=Ben Currie in, Alex Walmsley out in Rugby League World Cup opener|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/15623398.Currie_in__Walmsley_out|website=Warrington Guardian|access-date=10 January 2018|date=27 October 2017}}</ref>
